reviewer: agent build+matrix pass (runnability verified standalone AND on-page in headless Chrome; real non-silent Arabic audio; RTL rendering confirmed; desktop+mobile 0px overflow), revision 1

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY VERIFIED twice (headless Chrome, WASM, transformers.js 3.7.5). Standalone probe: pipeline('text-to-speech','Xenova/mms-tts-ara',{dtype:'q8'}) on an Arabic sentence returned a REAL Float32Array — 128000 samples @ 16000 Hz (8.0 s), peak amplitude 0.70, RMS 0.097, 98.8% non-zero (genuinely non-silent, not degenerate). On the built page: fresh profile showed the Download state + button, clicking it reached ready, and pressing تحدث produced real audio — 116,992 samples, 7.31 s, real-time factor 1.15×, player src set — with ZERO console errors.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a dedicated module Worker (new Worker(worker.js)); the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned) back to the page.

rubric.evidence: Expected output = { audio: Float32Array (mono), sampling_rate: 16000 }. The probe and the page both returned exactly that shape; the page encodes the Float32 PCM to a 16-bit WAV blob for <audio> and draws the same samples as the 'see inside' waveform. Non-silent Arabic speech (peak 0.70).
rubric.notes: A DISTINCT-language VITS demo: the Arabic checkpoint (own Arabic char vocab + Arabic-trained weights), materially different from the built English/German/Spanish mms-tts demos. Right-to-left script.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the exact model id (Xenova/mms-tts-ara, linked to its card), the original (facebook/mms-tts-ara), the task (text-to-speech / VitsModel), backend (WASM), quantisation (q8, model_quantized.onnx), size (~38 MB) and license (CC-BY-NC 4.0). Inference is off the main thread.
rubric.notes: CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial) noted in at-a-glance AND the footer byline.

rubric.evidence: The page loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture. Verified on-page: first visit (fresh profile) showed the Download-required state with a 'Download model (~38 MB)' button; clicking it reached state='ready' and enabled the Speak button.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/mms-tts-ara so the ~38 MB cache is tracked for the never-silently-re-download guarantee; a per-model clear-cache control is offered once ready.

rubric.evidence: Loading/ready/synthesising state is announced in role=status aria-live regions; the shared loader exposes a Retry recovery path on failure; a Synthesising…/Done status line reports progress. Console stayed clean across first-visit download.
rubric.notes: Errors surface on the page (status .err: 'Synthesis failed: …'), not only the console.

rubric.evidence: Every control is real and keyboard-operable: the RTL Arabic textarea (dir=rtl lang=ar), Arabic sample chips (ترحيب/تحية/الطقس/…), the تحدث (Speak) button (relabels to 'Speak again'), the <audio controls> element and the Download .wav link. Practical adds an Arabic register <select>; wild adds Generate/Add-one/Clear; multi-model chains the multilingual-toxicity gate + the TTS.
rubric.notes: Same proven control pattern as the built German/Spanish mms-tts demos.

rubric.evidence: No canned output — the worker runs the real transformers.js text-to-speech pipeline; the waveform, WAV and stats are all derived from the real Float32 samples. The standalone probe confirmed the exact non-silent Arabic audio the UI plays.

All buttons have accessible names; the canvas carries aria-label='Generated Arabic speech waveform'; sample chips are real buttons with ≥2.4rem min tap height; status uses role=status aria-live. RTL Arabic is set with dir=rtl + lang=ar and verified (computed direction:rtl, text-align:right) so the script renders correctly. Light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system.
rubric.notes: Logical CSS properties (inline-size, margin-inline, text-align:start) so the layout mirrors correctly; the .rtl class flips the text field for the Arabic script.

rubric.evidence: Verified 0px horizontal overflow at mobile (360×740, DPR3, touch) and desktop (1280×800) in headless Chrome across all five pages. The tts-grid collapses to one column ≤560px; audio-row + sample-row flex-wrap; textarea + canvas are inline-size:100%; the at-a-glance table uses table-layout:fixed + overflow-wrap:anywhere so the long model-id URL wraps instead of overflowing.
rubric.notes: Screenshots captured at both classes; support seeded via responsive-check.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; the audio buffer is transferred (not cloned). The waveform draw is a bounded per-pixel min/max reduction on a ~600px canvas (not per-sample), redrawn only on play/seek/resize — no main-thread inference or unbounded loop. Control UI stayed responsive during the 7.3 s synthesis.
rubric.notes: INP kept low — synthesis never blocks the interface.

guidanceConsulted.query: RTL bidirectional Arabic text rendering dir rtl lang; logical properties that flip in RTL
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Prefer logical properties/values so styles adapt to writing modes; ask 'would I want this to flip in RTL?' before using a logical property.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — Arabic text fields use dir=rtl + lang=ar (a .rtl class sets direction:rtl + text-align:right); layout uses inline-size/margin-inline/text-align:start so it mirrors. Verified computed direction=rtl on the text field.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: on-page probe: {dir:'rtl', computedDirection:'rtl', textAlign:'right', lang:'ar', hasArabic:true}

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel and audio player without horizontal overflow; long unbreakable URL in table
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap + min-inline-size:0 and intrinsic sizing; wrap long unbreakable tokens; avoid fixed widths.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — tts-grid single-column ≤560px; audio-row/sample-row flex-wrap; textarea+canvas inline-size:100%; at-a-glance table-layout:fixed + overflow-wrap:anywhere so the model-id URL wraps.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: 0px mobile overflow measured at 360×740 on all five pages in headless Chrome

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates for loading progress and canvas labelling
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; label every control; give the waveform canvas an accessible name.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — shared loader + page status use role=status aria-live; canvas has aria-label; sample chips and buttons carry accessible names.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

openQuestions: MMS-TTS Arabic expects undiacritised MSA; would adding an optional diacritisation note (or a second dialectal MMS Arabic variant) make pronunciation quality clearer to users?
Quantify the stochastic duration spread across Arabic sentence types (short announcements vs flowing proverbs) on the wild page.

followUpGoals.goal: Add another under-served MMS language demo (e.g. a low-resource language with no commercial TTS) to make the '1000+ languages, one recipe' claim tangible beyond Arabic/German/Spanish.
On the Arabic wild page, add a duration-spread histogram across sentence types to visualise where the stochastic duration predictor samples most freely.

summary: Revision-1 build+matrix pass for mms-tts-arabic (Xenova/mms-tts-ara, text-to-speech, q8, ~38 MB — the ARABIC VITS checkpoint of Meta MMS-TTS; a DISTINCT-language demo vs the built English/German/Spanish mms-tts: own Arabic char vocab + Arabic-trained weights, speaks the right-to-left Arabic script). RUNNABILITY VERIFIED standalone (128000 samples @16 kHz, peak 0.70, 98.8% non-zero, non-silent) AND on-page (first-visit Download → real 7.31 s Arabic audio, rtf 1.15×; ZERO console errors). RTL confirmed (computed direction:rtl, text-align:right). Ladder: basics (type Arabic, hear it), practical (Arabic narrator by register), wild (stochastic prosody lab), multi-model (multilingual-toxicity gate → only clean Arabic is spoken). 0px overflow desktop+mobile on all five pages. MMS weights are CC-BY-NC 4.0 (non-commercial), recorded in at-a-glance + footer.
